verdict_reasoning: TMED9 (p24alpha2) is a member of the p24/TMED family of type I transmembrane cargo receptors that cycle between the ER and Golgi apparatus. These proteins are resident endomembrane proteins that function in COPI/COPII vesicle trafficking and quality control within the early secretory pathway. The cytoplasmic tail faces the cytosol; the luminal domain faces the ER/Golgi lumen — NOT the extracellular space. No isoform is trafficked to the plasma membrane. Checking contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no evidence of stress/activation-induced PM display; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no evidence of surface display in any lineage; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal protein; (4) dual_localization — no documented PM pool; (5) stable_surface_attachment — not a secreted protein with surface anchoring. No therapeutic programs target TMED9 on the cell surface. The protein is firmly classified as an endomembrane-resident trafficking protein with no documented extracellular face exposure.

- HGNC approved name: transmembrane p24 trafficking protein 9
- HGNC symbol: TMED9
- UniProt accession: Q9BVK6
- Aliases: HSGP25L2G, p24alpha2, p24a2, GMP25, p25
- Previous symbols: (none)
- HGNC gene-group memberships: Transmembrane p24 trafficking proteins
- CD nomenclature: (none assigned)
- NCBI summary: This gene is a member of a family of genes encoding transport proteins located in the endoplasmic reticulum and the Golgi. A similar gene in mouse is the target of microRNA miR-296, which is part of an imprinted cluster. [provided by RefSeq, Jul 2016]
